title: cathartolinum pratense
side_effects: Cathartolinum pratense can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including diarrhea, stomach cramps, and nausea. In extreme cases, excessive use can lead to dehydration, electrolyte imbalance, and even kidney damage due to the plant's high saponin content. Additionally, improper use can cause allergic reactions, such as skin irritation, itching, and hives, in sensitive individuals. It is essential to consult a medical professional before using Cathartolinum pratense for medicinal purposes.
